mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC when tested against a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side momentum, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO prints above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of Fed rate hikes above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDPC1 revisions and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10), or forward returns show no directional bias following positive GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 3.5% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or the signal's hit rate drops below 48% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random when PAYEMS beats estimates but UNRATE rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing housing starts (HOUST) and contracting real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a demand shock that erodes corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive when both HOUST and GDPC1 print below their respective 3-month moving averages, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 2% in the subsequent month.
